subject: [COMMON] Enable importing custom certificates to truststore using certInitializer
commit_message:
[COMMON] Enable importing custom certificates to truststore using certInitializer

ONAP deployments may require the use of custom certificates. Instead of
manually adding certificates to the truststore file, users can now add
their .pem certificates under certInitializer/resources and have them
imported automatically by an init container. The updated truststore can
then be mounted to a component by providing a truststoreMountpath.

Issue-ID: OOM-2509
Signed-off-by: Jozsef Csongvai <jozsef.csongvai@bell.ca>
Change-Id: I896c729143346738e91fa57f895ba48043b253c1

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

Super small comments on my side, mainly on the license part.
good job!

As you'll have to make a new commit, please change the commit message in order to align with our rule:

the commit message (on OOM) must follow this form:

[NAME_OF_COMPONENT|DOC|COMMON|GENERIC] Meaningful title (from OOM side)

at least one sentence explaining the change done in this patch, cause and consequences and possibly more of course

Issue-ID: AS_WE_ARE_FORCED_BUT_MEANINGLESS
Change-ID: xxx
Sign-off: xxx

Commit message will be the last stuff that will stay with our code so it must clearly explain the changes, the "why" and the consequences. If it change OOM behavior in any way, documentation must be also updated.

Starting Frankfurt branching, merge requests which are not following this pattern will not be merged.

Please read the following pages and follow the guidelines for writing commit message contained therein.

    http://bit.ly/goodcommitmessages
    http://who-t.blogspot.com/2009/12/on-commit-messages.html
    http://dep.debian.net/deps/dep3/

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> It would be awesome if you could provide also some docummentation for this template explaining how you would like it to work as I'm not sure if you realize all the consequences of current code

I'm sure there are consequences that I don't fully realize, so any guidance you can provide will be much appreciated. Would you like more details in the commit message or where would that documentation go?

The main consequence that I anticipate, is that wherever certInitializer is used and this new feature is enabled - we will get an init container doing the work of importing certificates. I would love to have this done only once, but not sure how to do that (let me know if you have ideas). I did discuss this with Sebastien, and the conclusion is that this is still better than the way we manage certificates today by manually adding them to the truststore for the component that needs it. Is this the consequence you have in mind or are there others?

Please advise

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> > Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> > 
> > It would be awesome if you could provide also some docummentation for this template explaining how you would like it to work as I'm not sure if you realize all the consequences of current code
> 
> I'm sure there are consequences that I don't fully realize, so any guidance you can provide will be much appreciated. Would you like more details in the commit message or where would that documentation go?

We tend to document all the stuff in a comment above the template itself. You can check for example common secret template to see how we did this.

> 
> The main consequence that I anticipate, is that wherever certInitializer is used and this new feature is enabled - we will get an init container doing the work of importing certificates. I would love to have this done only once, but not sure how to do that (let me know if you have ideas). I did discuss this with Sebastien, and the conclusion is that this is still better than the way we manage certificates today by manually adding them to the truststore for the component that needs it. Is this the consequence you have in mind or are there others?

So by saying consequences I meant mostly how you intend to behave if AAF is enabled and how when it is disabled.
 
Just to make sure that we are on the same page: I've nothing against this patch apart from this small catch that I put in the comment. I just wanted to make sure that the way how I believe it works is exactly the thing that you expect from it in all conditions where this can be used;)
